email_parsing_patterns.description: Patterns and rules for identifying and extracting structured data from e-commerce emails. Used by Gemini 1.5 Flash to parse Gmail inbox.

shipment: Your package is on its way
delivery: Your Amazon.in order has been delivered

item_name.location: Usually in email body after order ID, in bold or heading
item_name.ai_prompt: Extract the product name from this order confirmation email. Look for the item title which is usually the most prominent text after the order number.

shipment: Your Flipkart order is on the way

item_name.location: Email body, usually as product title with brand name
item_name.ai_prompt: Extract the product name from this Flipkart order email. The product name is typically shown as a clickable link or bold text.

shipment: Your order is on its way

item_name.location: Product name with brand, usually styled with images
item_name.ai_prompt: Extract the product brand and name from this Myntra order email. Myntra emails typically show brand name prominently followed by product description.
Extract the product name from this Meesho order email.

item_name.location: Brand + product name in email body
item_name.ai_prompt: Extract the brand and product name from this AJIO order email.

shipment: Your order is on its way

item_name.ai_prompt: Extract the product brand and name from this Nykaa order email.

notification_patterns.description: Patterns for parsing Android notifications and SMS from e-commerce apps

sms_patterns.otp_filter: Ignore messages containing OTP, verification code, or login

gemini_master_prompt.description: The system prompt to send to Gemini 1.5 Flash for parsing any e-commerce email
gemini_master_prompt.prompt:
You are an AI email parser for Returnkart, an Indian e-commerce return tracking app. Your job is to extract structured order data from e-commerce emails.

Analyze the following email and extract data into this exact JSON format. If a field cannot be determined, use null.

```json
{
  "email_type": "order_confirmation | shipment | delivery | invoice | return_initiated | refund | other",
  "order_context": {
    "order_id": "string",
    "brand": "amazon_in | flipkart | myntra | meesho | ajio | nykaa | tatacliq | snapdeal | jiomart | other",
    "brand_display_name": "string",
    "item_name": "string",
    "item_category": "fashion | electronics | mobile | appliance | beauty | home | books | grocery | other",
    "total_amount": number,
    "currency": "INR",
    "quantity": number
  },
  "return_logic": {
    "policy_window_days": number,
    "expiry_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "is_replacement_only": boolean,
    "refund_type": "bank_refund | store_credit | replacement | null"
  },
  "logistics": {
    "delivery_pincode": "string or null",
    "courier_partner": "string or null",
    "tracking_id": "string or null",
    "order_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "expected_delivery_date": "YYYY-MM-DD or null",
    "actual_delivery_date": "YYYY-MM-DD or null"
  },
  "confidence_score": 0.0 to 1.0
}
```

IMPORTANT RULES:
1. For return_logic.policy_window_days, use the return policy dataset to look up the correct window based on brand + category.
2. Calculate expiry_date as: actual_delivery_date + policy_window_days.
3. If the email is not an e-commerce order/shipping/delivery email, return {"email_type": "other", "is_ecommerce": false}.
4. Always extract amounts in INR (Indian Rupees).
5. Normalize brand names to lowercase slugs.
6. confidence_score should reflect how certain you are about the extraction (1.0 = perfect match, 0.5 = some guessing).

gmail_search_queries.description: Gmail API search queries to find e-commerce emails

queries.name: All order confirmations (last 90 days)
queries.query: newer_than:90d (from:amazon.in OR from:flipkart.com OR from:myntra.com OR from:meesho.com OR from:ajio.com OR from:nykaa.com OR from:tatacliq.com OR from:snapdeal.com OR from:jiomart.com) (subject:order OR subject:invoice OR subject:shipped OR subject:delivered OR subject:return OR subject:refund)

queries.name: Amazon orders
queries.query: newer_than:90d from:amazon.in (subject:order OR subject:shipped OR subject:delivered OR subject:invoice)

queries.name: Flipkart orders
queries.query: newer_than:90d from:flipkart.com (subject:order OR subject:shipped OR subject:delivered)

queries.name: Myntra orders
queries.query: newer_than:90d from:myntra.com (subject:order OR subject:shipped OR subject:delivered)

queries.name: All returns and refunds
queries.query: newer_than:180d (from:amazon.in OR from:flipkart.com OR from:myntra.com OR from:meesho.com) (subject:return OR subject:refund OR subject:pickup)
